title: [Rotom] Battle Guide
summary: A fast [electric]/[ghost] special [pivot] that uses [levitate] to ignore g[round] moves while [volt-switch] cycles the tempo lead every turn.
weaknesses: Weak to dark and mirror [ghost] cove[rage]
Thin physical bulk gets cracked by priority
[choice-scarf] locks are easy to read and switch on

recommendedItems.reason: Scarves the into a speed-control piece
Mid-game heal to extend the cycles

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Rotom] is the rare Champions [pivot] that combines an [Electric]/[ghost] typing with the [levitate] ability. 95 SpA and 91 Speed are ordinary on paper, but the neutral cove[rage] of its STAB pair is huge and the g[round] immunity means switch math almost always favours it. The role is clean — bring it in on a g[round] attacker, c[lick] [volt-switch], and repeat until the opponent runs out of safe [pivot]s.

## Strengths
[levitate] wipes [earthquake] and [earth-power] off the threat list entirely, turning every g[round] attacker into a free switch opportunity. [thunderbolt] pressures [Water] and [Flying] cores, [shadow-ball] punches through [psychic] and [ghost] walls, and very few types resist both. [will-o-wisp] is the quiet workhorse of the set — one burn halves the damage of the oppo[sing] physical attacker and buys the backline breathing room.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Dark] and cove[rage] [ghost] moves bypass everything [Rotom] is good at, and with only 77/77 defences a [sing]le [shadow-sneak] can snap it in half when chipped. [Ghost] mirrors become a pure Speed race. [choice-scarf] sets trade flexibility for tempo — once the locked move is known, any g[round] type becomes a free switch, so telegraphing [thunderbolt] on a predictable turn is a [mist]ake.

## Recommended Moveset
[thunderbolt] is the locked [Electric] STAB and the strongest c[lick] on most match-ups. [shadow-ball] is the [Ghost] STAB that pries open [psychic] cores. [volt-switch] is the reason [Rotom] is on the team at all — c[lick] it whenever the opponent is about to switch to g[round], then replace [Rotom] with a partner that punishes that switch. The last slot goes to [will-o-wisp] to soften physical attackers; [trick] is a reasonable alternative against passive walls.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[choice-scarf] is the primary item, promoting [volt-switch] to speed control and locking [Rotom] above almost every unboosted threat. [magnet] is the offensive alternative when the team already has speed control elsewhere. [sitrus-berry] fits the long-game four-move build. The ability must be [levitate]. Tera [Electric] amplifies [thunderbolt]; Tera [Ghost] flips the dark weakness and [absorb]s a physical priority turn.

## Teammates & Synergy
Pair [Rotom] with a physically bulky tank that sponges the burnt attacker and the team breathes easier. [Fairy], [Normal], or [Steel] partners close the dark and [ghost] holes in the profile, and a hazard setter plus a dedicated [volt-switch] partner make the [pivot] loop sustainable across a long game.

## Play Patterns
[Rotom] leads comfortably and also comes in on turn two once the oppo[sing] g[round] is known. C[lick] [volt-switch] the moment a g[round] switch is telegraphed, and stash [will-o-wisp] for the first physical threat that enters. Hold Tera until a dark or [ghost] STAB is about to connect — that [sing]le turn of flipped weaknesses usually buys another full [pivot] rotation.
